title: General Aptitude - Verbal Aptitude - Verbal Reasoning

questions.question:
The question below consists of a pair of related words followed by four pairs of words. Select the pair that best expresses the relation in the original pair.

\( \text{QUISLING : BETRAY} \)



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2021 Dec Scientist A

The question below consists of a pair of related words followed by four pairs of words. Select the pair that best expresses the relation in the original pair.

\( \text{INTIMATE : CLOSE} \)



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2021 Dec Scientist A

Arrange the given words in alphabetical order and tick the one that comes at the second place.



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section A | Question: 14

Choose the word which is least like the other words in the group :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section A | Question: 21

Hypsiphobia : Height : : Hylophobia : ?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section A | Question: 34

Reluctant: Keen: : Remarkable:?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist C | Section A | Question: 11

Directions : \( (28-29) \) In each of the following letter series, some of the letters are missing which are given in that order as one of the alternatives below it. Choose the correct alternative.

\( \text{pqr _ _ rs_rs_ _ s_q_} \)



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist C | Section A | Question: 29

Direction: \( (31-34) \) In the following questions find the appropriate explanation for idioms/phrases mentioned in bold letters.

I feel that I have been left up the creek without a paddle by my father.



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist C | Section B | Question: 31

Direction \( (38-40) \): Choose the correct substitute for the following phrases/sentences.

A political leader who seeks support by appealing to popular desires and prejudices rather than by using rational argument :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist C | Section B | Question: 40

Direction \( (47-49) \): Read the following comprehension carefully and answer the following questions.
Humans are to be blamed for declining bat populations. Bats get poisoned when they consume insects that have been sprayed with pesticides and insecticides. But the biggest problem for bats is the loss of natural habitat. Many bats prefer to roost in dead or dying trees under the loose and peeling bark, or in tree cavities. Some prefer to roost in caverns. Populations have dwindled and diversity has suffered without the protection of these important natural roosts.

Which is an appropriate and accurate explication of this paragraph ?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist C | Section B | Question: 48
options: Bats would thrive if humans did not use synthetic pesticides.
Responsible citizens should construct friendly habitats for wildlife.
Humans have unintentionally contributed to declining bat populations.
Bats must seek roosts in caves and caverns when other roosts are unavailable.
questions.correct_answer: Humans have unintentionally contributed to declining bat populations.

questions.question:
Rearrange the letters given below to form a meaningful word and select from the given alternatives the word which is almost opposite in meaning to the word so formed :

\( \text{RBANOEHCER} \)



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section A | Question: 4

Directions : (5-6) The following questions consist of two words each, that have a certain relationship with each other, followed by four lettered pairs of words. Select the letter pair that has the same relationship as the original pair of words.

Mendacity : Honesty



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section A | Question: 5

Directions : (5-6) The following questions consist of two words each, that have a certain relationship with each other, followed by four lettered pairs of words. Select the letter pair that has the same relationship as the original pair of words.

Identity: Anonymity



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section A | Question: 6

Directions: In questions (35 - 36), the sentences have been given in Direct / Indirect speech. From the given alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the given sentence in direct or indirect speech as directed:

Change the following sentence into indirect speech :

"Who used the secondary source?"



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 35
options: The secondary source was used by me.
She asked who is using the secondary source.
She enquired who had used the secondary source.
She wanted to know who all were reading the secondary source.
questions.correct_answer: She enquired who had used the secondary source.

questions.question:
Directions: In questions (39-42) out of the four alternatives choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence.

An 'extra-terrestrial' is :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 41
options: Originating on earth but capable of travelling into space.
Something that visits the earth or it's atmosphere.
Originating, existing, or occurring outside the earth or its atmosphere.
questions.correct_answer: Originating, existing, or occurring outside the earth or its atmosphere.

questions.question:
Directions: In questions (43-46), rearrange the following five sentences \( \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{S} \) and \( \mathrm{T} \) in the proper sequence to form a meaningful paragraph; then answer the questions given below them.

\( \mathrm{P} \). With the death of Queen Anne in August \( 1714 \) and the accession of George I, the Tories were a ruined party, and Swift's career in England was at an end.

\( \mathrm{Q} \). A Tory ministry headed by Robert Harley (later earl of Oxford) and Henry St. John (later Viscount Bolingbroke) was replacing that of the Whigs.

\( \mathrm{R} \). The astute Harley made overtures to Swift and won him over to the Tories.

\( \mathrm{S} \). Swift quickly became the Tories' chief pamphleteer and political writer and, by the end of October \( 1710 \), had taken over the Tory journal, The Examiner, which he continued to edit until June \( 14, 1711 \).

\( \mathrm{T} \). A momentous period began for Swift when in \( 1710 \) he once again found himself in London.

Which of the sentences should come fourth in the sequence?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 43

Directions: In questions (43-46), rearrange the following five sentences \( \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R}, \mathrm{S} \) and \( \mathrm{T} \) in the proper sequence to form a meaningful paragraph; then answer the questions given below them.

\( \mathrm{P} \). With the death of Queen Anne in August \( 1714 \) and the accession of George I, the Tories were a ruined party, and Swift's career in England was at an end.

\( \mathrm{Q} \). A Tory ministry headed by Robert Harley (later earl of Oxford) and Henry St. John (later Viscount Bolingbroke) was replacing that of the Whigs.

\( \mathrm{R} \). The astute Harley made overtures to Swift and won him over to the Tories.

\( \mathrm{S} \). Swift quickly became the Tories' chief pamphleteer and political writer and, by the end of October \( 1710 \), had taken over the Tory journal, The Examiner, which he continued to edit until June \( 14, 1711 \).

\( \mathrm{T} \). A momentous period began for Swift when in \( 1710 \) he once again found himself in London.

Which of the sentences should come fifth in the sequence?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 44

Directions: In questions (54 - 55) choose the alternative that is closest to the meaning of the italicized part of the sentence from the options given below.

Since you didn't.study hard, I am afraid you will have to face the music.



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 55

Directions (56 to 59): Identify which part of the sentence contains error and mark it as your answer.

The capital of Himachal Pradesh / is situating / \( 2205 \) meters above / the sea level.



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section B | Question: 56

Human technology developed from the first stone tools about two and a half million years ago. At the beginning, the rate of development was slow. Hundreds of thousands of years passed without much change. Today, new technologies are reported daily on television and in newspapers.

This paragraph best supports the statement that:



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section C | Question: 83
options: Stone tools were in use for two and a half million years.
There is no way to know when stone tools first came into use.
In today's world, new technologies are constantly being developed.
questions.correct_answer: In today's world, new technologies are constantly being developed.

questions.question:
In the \( 1966 \) Supreme Court decision Miranda v. Arizona, the court held that before the police can obtain statements from a person subjected to an interrogation, the person must be given a Miranda warning. This warning means that a person must be told that he or she has the right to remain silent during the police interrogation. Violation of this right means that any statement that the person makes is not admissible in a court hearing.

This paragraph best supports the statement that :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section C | Question: 84
options: Police who do not warn persons of their Miranda rights are guilty of a crime.
A Miranda warning must be given before a police interrogation can begin.
The police may no longer interrogate persons suspected of a crime unless a lawyer is present.
Persons who are interrogated by police should always remain silent until their lawyer comes.
questions.correct_answer: A Miranda warning must be given before a police interrogation can begin.

questions.question:
Yoga has become a very popular type of exercise, but it may not be for everyone. Before you sign yourself up for a yoga class, you need to examine what it is you want from your fitness routine. If you're looking for a high-energy, fast-paced aerobic workout, a yoga class might not be your best choice.

This paragraph best supports the statement that :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section C | Question: 85
options: Yoga is more popular than high-impact aerobics.
Before embarking on a new exercise regimen, you should think about your needs and desires.
Yoga is changing the world of fitness in major ways.
questions.correct_answer: Before embarking on a new exercise regimen, you should think about your needs and desires.

questions.question:
During colonial times in America, juries were encouraged to ask questions of the parties in the courtroom. The jurors were, in fact, expected to investigate the facts of the case themselves. If jurors conducted an investigation today, we would throw out the case.

This paragraph best supports the statement that :



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section C | Question: 86
options: Juries are less important today than they were in colonial times.
Courtrooms today are more efficient than they were in colonial times.
Jurors in colonial times were more informed than jurors today.
The jury system in America has changed since colonial times.
questions.correct_answer: The jury system in America has changed since colonial times.

questions.question:
Mathematics allows us to expand our consciousness. Mathematics tells us about economic trends, patterns of disease, and the growth of populations. Math is good at exposing the truth, but it can also perpetuate misunderstandings and untruths. Figures have the power to mislead people. This paragraph best supports the statement that:



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section C | Question: 87
options: Figures are sometimes used to deceive people.
The study of mathematics is more important than other disciplines.
The power of numbers is that they cannot lie.
questions.correct_answer: Figures are sometimes used to deceive people.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about behaviour and attitudes is \( \text{NOT} \) true?



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section E | Question: 134
options: Attitudes that are easily remembered are more likely to predict behaviour than attitudes that are not accessible in memory.
Attitudes that individuals consider important tend to show a strong relationship to behaviour.
The attitude-behaviour relationship is likely to be much stronger if an attitude refers to something with which the individual has direct personal experience.
General attitudes have a greater influence on behaviour than specific attitudes.
questions.correct_answer: General attitudes have a greater influence on behaviour than specific attitudes.

questions.question:
In an organization, one can gain valuable perspectives and insights through close association with an experienced person willing to take one under his/her wing. Such an individual is often called a __________.



Verbal Aptitude: NIELIT 2023 Feb Scientist D | Section E | Question: 149
